features.name: Strategy trust calibration and extreme-risk gates
capabilities: Historical 5m/15m/1h signal backtest metrics
Confidence calibrated by historical quality and risk penalties
Realtime-prejudge versus closed-candle confirmation labels
Extreme ATR/BOLL risk gates for small volatile coins
Entry trigger confirmation using 1m volume, structure, spread, and entry distance
Tick-size legal stop hints and grid stops
Separate direction quality score from executable opening score
Global no-open gate propagation across all timeframe cards
ATR-based near-entry trigger threshold plus 1m retest confirmation
Stop-first historical backtest path accounting with estimated fee/slippage cost
Backtest stop-out count, stop rate, average loss, net expectancy, and estimated cost fields
Funding-rate crowding risk gate downgrade
Order-book depth top5/top20 and depth imbalance in trigger confirmation
Unclosed 1m trigger candles forced to watch instead of confirmed
Strong frontend warnings for no-open gates, half-position bans, realtime-prejudge, and position conflicts
Shared online/backtest side threshold for signal selection
Backtest filtered-out sample count and ATR/volume filter note
Gross expectancy versus net expectancy after estimated fee/slippage
Price-path drawdown kept separate from execution cost
Reusable runtime backtest cache for analyzer/server calls
Realtime SSE depth20 stream with depth imbalance and top5/top20 depth
API error classification for invalid symbols, rate limits, timeouts, network errors, and upstream failures
Partial-symbol API responses are rejected with missing/returned symbol metadata instead of cached as successful snapshots
Backtest cache writes use lock-file protection and unique temp files for concurrent analyzer subprocesses
TLM extreme-rally downgrade is applied before side, backtest, and stop selection, with downgraded direction-score caps
Backend risk-budget sizing with suggested_size_pct, stop_distance_pct, max_loss_pct, and allowed/note metadata
Frontend account-risk fuse for daily loss, consecutive losses, and max single loss, blocking new-open suggestions until next local day
Frontend position display uses risk-budget percentage instead of score-to-star position labels
Same-side altcoin correlation exposure warning across loaded symbols
Frontend stale/warning/error_type handling for delayed cached snapshots
Realtime depth fields are preserved across bookTicker-only updates and strategy refreshes
verification: python -B -m py_compile src/bian_dashboard/analyzer.py src/bian_dashboard/server.py bian.py server.py
GET /api/market?symbols=DOGEUSDT,TLMUSDT includes risk_sizing for top signal and every timeframe advice
GET /api/realtime-prices?symbols=DOGEUSDT streams depth_imbalance and top5 depth fields
limitations: Historical backtest is still a lightweight rolling signal-quality check; it now estimates fees/slippage and stop-first path, but does not model funding windows, liquidation, partial fills, queue position, account equity, or portfolio exposure.
Backtest samples are filtered by ATR/volume and should not be interpreted as all-market win rate.
Signals are suitable for assisted monitoring, not autonomous high-confidence trade execution.
